things.name: Petronas Twin Towers
things.description: The world's tallest twin towers (452m) with a sky bridge on the 41st floor and KLCC shopping below

things.name: Batu Caves
things.description: Dramatic limestone hill with a 43-metre golden Lord Murugan statue and sacred Hindu temple caves

things.name: Merdeka Square & Sultan Abdul Samad Building
things.description: The vast square where Malaysia declared independence in 1957, flanked by Moorish colonial architecture

things.name: Jalan Alor Night Food Street
things.description: KL's most famous street food strip — dozens of open-air restaurants serving hawker food until 2am

things.name: Islamic Arts Museum
things.description: Southeast Asia's largest Islamic arts museum with architectural models, textiles and stunning domes

things.name: KL Tower (Menara KL)
things.description: The world's seventh tallest telecommunications tower with observation decks and an open-air Sky Deck

things.name: Chinatown (Petaling Street)
things.description: Colourful night market with replica goods, dim sum and the atmospheric Chan See Shu Yuen Temple

things.name: Little India (Brickfields)
things.description: KL's colourful Indian district with garland shops, curry houses, sari shops and vibrant street art

things.name: KLCC Park & Skyline Walk
things.description: Beautifully landscaped park beneath the Twin Towers with pools, waterfall and reflection views at night

things.name: Genting Highlands Day Trip
things.description: Malaysia's cool-weather mountain resort 45 km from KL with the Chin Swee Caves Temple and theme park
